title: Full body waifus, AI dreams, realtime AI music, open-source Gemini Omni: AI NEWS
summary: A comprehensive AI news roundup covering major model releases and tools including Minimax M3, Ideogram v4, Gemma4, Nemotron 3 Ultra, and over 20 other AI systems. The video highlights advances in image generation, language models, music generation, robotics, and reasoning agents across both open-source and proprietary platforms.
writeup: A weekly roundup of open-source AI releases spanning video, 3D reconstruction, music, and memory, with most models available to run locally. The through-line is the breadth of capabilities now shipping as open weights: ByteDance's Bernini unifies text, image, and video references for flexible video editing; Nvidia's Deja Vu reconstructs 3D scenes from multiple images at only 117 million parameters; Google's Magenta Real-Time 2 drops music generation latency from 3 seconds to 200 milliseconds; and OpenAI's ChatGPT Dreaming feature synthesizes memory across past conversations in the background to keep context from going stale. Each release is covered with quantitative benchmarks and local-run instructions where available.
key_points: ByteDance's Bernini is an open-source unified video-editing model that accepts text, image, and video references simultaneously, supports operations including background swap, perspective change, character insertion, and style transfer, and requires roughly 84 GB per branch on Hugging Face, with ComfyUI integration in progress.

Nvidia's Deja Vu performs 3D Gaussian-splat reconstruction from multiple images by reusing a single transformer block in repeated passes rather than stacking layers, achieving performance that matches Depth Anything 3 (which is roughly 10 times larger) at only 117 million parameters and 468 MB.

Google Magenta Real-Time 2 is a 2.4-billion-parameter live music generator that responds to MIDI, audio, and text in real time with 200 ms latency (down from 3 seconds in version 1), runs on a MacBook without a GPU, and is aimed at use as a DAW plugin rather than a standalone text-to-song tool.

ChatGPT's new Dreaming feature synthesizes persistent memory from past conversations in the background rather than relying on static saved notes, improving task-accuracy and factual-recall metrics and correctly updating context when circumstances change, such as recognizing that a completed trip is over; the feature is rolling out to free users.

The Mama markerless motion-capture model takes multi-view video and produces per-person 3D pose, shape, hand, and motion estimates for multiple people simultaneously, trained on a synthetic dataset called Mama Sin with 2.5 million crops covering extreme poses and contact interactions.
